question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerSystemFile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the tiny maximumSize=20 of the systemFile cache, where issuing requests that reference more than 20 distinct system file keys causes constant cache thrashing, and since system files are critical for EVM fee schedule and network parameter lookups, their repeated DB re-fetching under load causes request timeouts and service degradation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerSystemFileExchangeRates()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the maximumSize=20 constraint of the exchangeRate cache, where issuing requests that force more than 20 distinct exchange rate cache keys causes constant eviction, and since exchange rates are used for every gas price calculation, their unavailability from cache causes every single contract call to perform a DB lookup, saturating the database?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerContractState()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by crafting eth_call requests that read from a large number of distinct storage slots across many contracts, where the contractState cache (maximumSize=25000) is filled with attacker-controlled slot values, and the 2-second TTL causes all 25000 entries to expire and be re-queried from the DB every 2 seconds, creating a sustained 12500 DB queries/second load?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by issuing requests that resolve EVM addresses (CACHE_NAME_EVM_ADDRESS) and aliases (CACHE_NAME_ALIAS) for 10000+ distinct values, filling the entity cache and causing the 1-second TTL to trigger mass expiration, forcing the DB to handle 10000 entity lookups per second continuously, which exceeds the DB connection pool capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by issuing historical eth_call requests with 10000+ distinct timestamp values, filling the recordFileTimestamp cache (expireAfterAccess=10m, maximumSize=10000) with attacker-controlled entries, evicting legitimate timestamp-to-block mappings and causing all subsequent legitimate historical queries to miss the cache and perform expensive DB timestamp range scans?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerContractSlots()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the enableBatchContractSlotCaching=true default in CacheProperties, where issuing a single eth_call request that reads many storage slots triggers a batch DB query that populates the contractSlots cache (maximumSize=3000) with a large number of entries at once, and by repeating this with different contracts, the attacker can continuously evict and re-populate the cache, causing sustained DB batch query load?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerToken()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by issuing NFT allowance queries (CACHE_NAME_NFT_ALLOWANCE) and token airdrop queries (CACHE_NAME_TOKEN_AIRDROP) with thousands of distinct token/account combinations, filling the shared token cache (maximumSize=10000) and causing the 1-second TTL to trigger mass expiration, forcing the DB to handle the full query load without caching benefit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileEarliest()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that cacheManagerRecordFileEarliest has maximumSize=1 and no expiry, meaning if the single cached entry is populated with an incorrect or boundary-case block value during a race condition at startup, all subsequent requests that depend on the earliest block number will receive wrong data, causing EVM historical simulation to fail for all users indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerContractState()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by issuing eth_call requests that read from contract storage slots with keys crafted to maximize hash collisions in the Caffeine cache's internal hash table, degrading cache lookup performance from O(1) to O(n) and causing all contract state reads to become CPU-intensive, starving other requests of CPU time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheManagerSlotsPerContract()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by issuing requests against contracts with entity IDs that produce very long string representations (used as cache names in cacheManagerSlotsPerContract), causing the CaffeineCacheManager to allocate large string keys for each dynamic cache, consuming excessive heap memory and triggering frequent GC pauses that degrade service performance?
